coreJobStatement: Provide paid temporary accommodation that meets guests' comfort, safety, and accessibility needs while maintaining full regulatory compliance with fire safety and building codes
productJobStatement: Help Building Security & Life Safety to detect and deter unauthorized use of emergency exit doors while maintaining unimpeded fire-code-compliant escape capability across all floors and wings
lPath: L7 Hotel Ecosystem > L6H-SEC Building Security & Life Safety > L5 Emergency Exit Security Devices

segments.name: Multi-property hotel chain (portfolio)
segments.characteristics: Manages 10-500+ properties with centralized security oversight
segments.targetabilityReason: Needs cross-property monitoring aggregation; individual property economics differ
segments.circumstance: Manages 10-500+ properties with centralized security oversight
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — needs portfolio-level aggregation (A3 IoT only viable option)

segments.name: Full-service / upscale single property
segments.characteristics: High brand-standard scrutiny; architect-designed interiors; integrated BMS common
segments.targetabilityReason: Aesthetic sensitivity (C5 surface mount friction); BMS protocol integration required (C6)
segments.circumstance: High brand-standard scrutiny; architect-designed interiors; integrated BMS common
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — aesthetic sensitivity and BMS integration requirements filter alternatives to A2 or A3

segments.name: Select-service / mid-scale property
segments.characteristics: Retrofit budget approved; cost-ROI driven; limited in-house facilities staff
segments.targetabilityReason: Commissioning ease and IoT dashboard value proposition are primary evaluation criteria
segments.circumstance: Retrofit budget approved; cost-ROI driven; limited in-house facilities staff
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — commissioning ease and IoT dashboard value are primary evaluation criteria

segments.name: Budget / limited-service motel
segments.characteristics: Minimal infrastructure; no BMS; price-sensitive; owner-operated
segments.targetabilityReason: IoT premium (C7) is barrier; mechanical-only may dominate; simple deployment required
segments.circumstance: Minimal infrastructure; no BMS; price-sensitive; owner-operated
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — IoT premium is barrier; A1 mechanical alarm or A5 do-nothing dominates

segments.name: Extended-stay property
segments.characteristics: Semi-residential guest tenure (7-30+ days); reduced after-hours staffing
segments.targetabilityReason: Battery life more critical (long cycles between maintenance); semi-public exit use patterns
segments.circumstance: Semi-residential guest tenure (7-30+ days); reduced after-hours staffing
segments.alternativesDiffer: Yes — battery life and reduced staffing shift value toward IoT remote monitoring (A3)

alternatives.name: Basic mechanical door alarm (no monitoring) — 45% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: No remote visibility; staff must physically patrol to detect unreported propping; no audit log; battery-free

alternatives.name: Wired panic bar / crash bar alarm — 20% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Requires mains wiring conduit to each door; high retrofit cost in existing buildings; no wireless aggregation

alternatives.name: Alarmed exit hardware with IoT monitoring (Türwächter) — 25% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Proprietary wireless protocol (C6); surface-mount profile visible (C5); battery dependency (C4); ~730 EUR per door (C7)

alternatives.name: Security guard patrols
alternatives.tradeoffs: Labor-intensive; coverage gaps at shift changes; no event log; scales linearly with door count; high recurring cost

alternatives.name: No dedicated exit monitoring (do nothing) — 10% share
alternatives.tradeoffs: Zero installation burden; zero P1 burden; full compliance and brand audit risk; undetected misuse propagates

jobSteps.description: which emergency exit doors require monitoring, alert thresholds, notification rules, and coverage zones
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Coverage scope, alert threshold, and notification rule configuration
jobSteps.rawStatement: Determine which emergency exit doors require monitoring, alert thresholds, notification rules, and coverage zones

jobSteps.description: all emergency exit points across floors; assess wireless signal paths; map gateway placement
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Wireless RF coverage mapping and gateway placement
jobSteps.rawStatement: Identify all emergency exit points across floors; assess wireless signal paths; map gateway placement
jobSteps.rationale: RF coverage planning — gateway and device placement determines system effectiveness

jobSteps.description: Install monitoring devices on door handles; pair to gateway; configure zones; test alarm tone
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Device installation, gateway pairing, zone configuration
jobSteps.rawStatement: Install monitoring devices on door handles; pair to gateway; configure zones; test alarm tone
jobSteps.rationale: Commissioning — physical installation and wireless pairing determine deployment speed

jobSteps.description: Verify every device transmits correctly, alarm is unobstructed, and escape motion is unrestricted
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Acceptance testing of alarm and radio transmission
jobSteps.rawStatement: Verify every device transmits correctly, alarm is unobstructed, and escape motion is unrestricted
jobSteps.rationale: Acceptance testing — validates alarm function and wireless transmission before go-live

jobSteps.description: Maintain all exits in secured state 24/7; trigger alert on unauthorized activation
jobSteps.rawStatement: Maintain all exits in secured state 24/7; trigger alert on unauthorized activation
jobSteps.rationale: Core function — the primary security action the product performs continuously

jobSteps.description: Track real-time exit door status from centralized dashboard; receive alerts; correlate with CCTV or BMS
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Centralized dashboard and alert delivery — primary IoT value
jobSteps.rawStatement: Track real-time exit door status from centralized dashboard; receive alerts; correlate with CCTV or BMS
jobSteps.rationale: Primary IoT value — centralized visibility replaces physical patrol

jobSteps.description: Respond to and reset triggered devices; adjust alert rules; replace failed units
jobSteps.sensorDependencyRationale: Alert response, device reset, rule adjustment
jobSteps.rawStatement: Respond to and reset triggered devices; adjust alert rules; replace failed units
jobSteps.rationale: Operational — alert response workflow and device lifecycle management

jobSteps.description: Generate compliance audit reports; log fire marshal inspections; replace low-battery units proactively
jobSteps.rawStatement: Generate compliance audit reports; log fire marshal inspections; replace low-battery units proactively
jobSteps.rationale: Compliance output — audit documentation and preventive maintenance scheduling

needs.statement: Minimize the likelihood of high-risk emergency exits remaining unmonitored due to incomplete upfront coverage mapping, leaving >=1 undetected propping event per month
Increase the ability to determine wireless receiver placement achieving >95% floor-level signal coverage before hardware purchase or installation
Minimize the time required to install and commission emergency exit monitoring devices across a 100-room hotel to <4 hours without structural door modification
Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit alarm functionality being impaired during device mounting, pairing, or initial configuration

Increase the ability to verify that every installed exit monitoring device is transmitting correctly and that the audible alarm operates without obstruction — within a single end-to-end commissioning test
Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit remaining in an unauthorized open state for >5 minutes without triggering a staff alert
Minimize the likelihood of legitimate authorized staff uses of emergency exits (housekeeping, maintenance) generating false alerts that erode security staff confidence in the monitoring system
Minimize the likelihood of an emergency exit monitoring device adding any additional step or force requirement that delays occupant evacuation during a fire emergency

Increase the ability of a single security officer to monitor real-time exit door status across all floors from one centralized interface without physical patrol — covering >=50 exit points simultaneously
Minimize the time from emergency exit activation event to security staff notification to <30 seconds regardless of time of day
Minimize the likelihood of wireless connectivity loss causing undetected monitoring gaps exceeding 15 minutes during hotel occupied hours
Minimize the likelihood of the monitoring system misclassifying an emergency evacuation event as an unauthorized door use, triggering an incorrect security response that delays fire evacuation
Minimize the time required to reset a triggered exit monitoring device and restore it to active state after investigation, to <2 minutes
Minimize the likelihood of a high-priority unauthorized exit event being missed due to alert saturation from high-frequency low-priority activations
Increase the ability to produce a complete, timestamped digital audit log of all emergency exit activations and response actions, formatted for fire marshal inspection without manual compilation
Minimize the time required to identify and proactively replace batteries approaching depletion threshold across a deployment of >=30 exit monitoring devices before monitoring capability is lost
Minimize the effort required to integrate emergency exit monitoring event data with an existing hotel BMS or security management platform running standard building protocols
Minimize the variability in emergency exit compliance status across all floor levels at any given time of day
Minimize the likelihood of emergency exit door management violations being cited during annual fire authority inspection or franchise brand standards audit
Increase the ability to demonstrate evidence-based emergency exit management to insurance underwriters and brand auditors with a single report without manual data assembly
Minimize the likelihood of a preventable emergency exit failure becoming a documented finding in a franchise brand standards audit
Minimize the sense of uncertainty about whether unmanned emergency exits remain secured during overnight minimum-staffing periods
